title: narcissus pseudonarcissus subsp. muticus
intro: Narcissus pseudonarcissus subsp. muticus, also known as the wild daffodil, is a subspecies of the common daffodil plant, characterized by its trumpet-shaped flowers and green leaves. The plant is used in herbal medicine for its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been traditionally used to relieve respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The health benefits of Narcissus pseudonarcissus subsp. muticus include its ability to reduce inflammation, soothe respiratory issues, and even exhibit antiviral and antibacterial properties. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to treat respiratory conditions and other health issues.
